bullets: Engineered an automated Python data pipeline to ingest and process unstructured text from IBM OpenPages, implementing advanced NLP preprocessing (tokenization, regex parsing, and text normalization) to convert raw regulatory data into structured feature vectors for RCM classification.

Trained and evaluated Hugging Face models to generate accurate semantic embeddings, operationalizing the Python data pipeline as an Azure Machine Learning (AML) Studio endpoint to stream contextual data to an OpenAI model.

Architected and deployed scalable Azure cloud infrastructure using Terraform IaC, contributing to internal open-source development models (InnerSource) to deliver reusable infrastructure modules across collaborative engineering teams.

Designed and optimized robust CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps, integrating automated Veracode and SonarQube static analysis to enforce strict security guardrails and enhance overall code quality across platform components.
Executed comprehensive ablation studies across XGBoost, LightGBM, LSTM, and Transformer architectures, validating the stability of differentiable surrogate loss functions for time-series forecasting.

Generated critical experimental results demonstrating a 33–50% reduction in Peak Mean Absolute Error (MAE) at 48-hour horizons, providing the empirical basis for the paper's core claims on optimization stability.
Quantified trade-offs between predictive accuracy and operational safety via sensitivity analysis on asymmetric risk parameters, mitigating underprediction bias in critical risk-forecasting models.

Designed a modular Python-based evaluation framework to automate hyperparameter tuning and model versioning, ensuring 100% reproducibility across distributed training runs on NCSU's high-performance computing (HPC) cluster.
Optimized full-stack performance by systematically troubleshooting integration bottlenecks between React frontends and Django backends, reducing page load latency by 3 seconds.
Architected a scalable PostgreSQL database and Django REST API ecosystem, enforcing data integrity through advanced ORM constraints, validations, and atomic transactions to streamline data flows.

Formulated a comprehensive test automation framework by authoring backend unit tests with pytest and frontend integration tests for React components, establishing rigorous quality gates to prevent regression defects.
Utilized Git and GitHub for version control, actively participating in team code reviews, pull request workflows, and continuous integration practices to maintain codebase stability.
Led lab sessions and provided technical support for 50+ students in E298: Applied AI for Engineering, covering machine learning, deep learning, and AI applications.
Guided students in applying AI/ML concepts, Python, and OpenAI LLMs to solve real-world engineering challenges, improving project outcomes by an estimated third of a letter grade.
Engineered features for a policy search portal using Azure AI Search, implementing semantic search capabilities that improved result relevance ranking by 15% compared to keyword-based baselines.

Developed an automated web scraping solution using Python and Selenium to navigate complex government portals, reliably extracting and downloading critical regulatory documents for internal processing.
Deployed and managed search application components on Microsoft Azure, gaining hands-on experience with cloud-native data services and scalable architecture patterns.
Collaborated within an Agile Scrum team to refine product requirements, translating user needs into technical specifications for data-driven features.
Developed an interactive data dashboard using Python (NumPy, Pandas), CSS, and Streamlit for public health officials to interpret research findings.
Migrated from static Matplotlib charts to interactive Altair and Plotly visualizations, standardizing text to approximately a 7th-grade reading level.
Cleaned and validated raw toxicological data from multiple sources using Pandas, ensuring data integrity for the final dashboard.
Presented finalized data narratives to senior toxicologists, facilitating identification of regional pollutant trends for statewide public health recommendations.
Achieved a top 25 global finish (out of 100+ entrants) in the RoboSub competition by developing real-time movement and computer vision algorithms for an autonomous underwater robot.
Engineered a computer vision pipeline in Rust with ONNX models that improved autonomous object deployment accuracy by 25% during competitive trials.
GPA: 3.70/4.00 | Certification: Security+
Activities: AI/ML Teaching Assistant, HackPack, AquaPack Robotics
Relevant Courses: C and Software Tools, Data Structures and Algorithms, DevOps, Python Applications, Operating Systems, Software Engineering, Software Security
